citations.description: _code_section handles code_n parameter; code round selection is implemented
AC7 test seeds ≥2 plan rounds and tests plan_round=2 — no code_round=2 test present

Searched all test files for 'code_round=2', 'two code', 'multiple code_review', 'OLD-CODE' and '2 code-review rounds' — no results found. No test seeds ≥2 code-review rounds and selects the older one.

findings.criterion: The page is navigable across the retained review history for BOTH plan review AND code review: an OLDER round (not just the latest) of EACH can be selected and its distinct content renders. Oracle: a live end-to-end test seeding ≥2 plan-review rounds AND ≥2 code-review rounds on one ticket, selecting the older round in each gate, and asserting each renders its (distinct from latest) content.
findings.detail: The implementation in `page.py` supports `code_round` navigation (`_code_section` handles the `code_n` parameter and selects sidecars by 1-based index; `server.py` accepts `code_round` as a query parameter), and plan-round navigation is tested by `test_round_selector_default_latest_and_older` (seeds 2 plan rounds, selects `plan_round=2`, asserts distinct content). However, the oracle specifically requires a test seeding ≥2 code-review rounds AND selecting the older code-review round. No such test exists: `test_audit_ticket_page_heldout.py` AC7 seeds only plan rounds and only tests `?plan_round=2`. No test file seeds 2 code-review sidecars and asserts that `?code_round=2` renders the older code-review round's distinct content.
findings.remediation: Add a test seeding ≥2 code-review rounds on one ticket (e.g. by calling emit_code_review twice or emitting two sidecars on the same artifact ticket), then requesting `/ticket/<id>?code_round=2` and asserting the older round's distinct finding content is rendered and differs from the latest.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
